title: Herbivory and Physical Damage Module. In 'Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual using the Ecological Monitoring System Australia'
author: Cox, Bethany
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This module is one of many that form the  Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual  using the  Ecological Monitoring System Australia (EMSA),  available at  https://emsa.tern.org.au/documents . The Herbivory and Physical Damage Module outlines the procedures and guidelines of three protocols for recording herbivory and physical damage present in a project area: 
 
 Within-plot belt transect protocol 
 Off-plot belt transect protocol 
 Active plot search protocol 
 
 The module is appropriate for monitoring all fauna species, including pest species. Depending on the project aims and location, herbivory and physical damage surveys can consist of a single protocol or a combination of a belt transect protocol and the Active plot search protocol. Where possible, protocols of the Herbivory and Physical Damage Module should be undertaken within or near an established 1 ha Core Monitoring Plot or Fauna Plot so that the data can be analysed in combination with vegetation and landform data collected.
